barks.text: Almost. The string wants to be somewhere, just not there.
barks.speaker: gary

barks.text: Grandpa always said a hunch is a fact you haven't earned yet. This one I haven't even met.
barks.speaker: gary

barks.text: The string sags like it's embarrassed for me. Fair.
barks.speaker: gary

barks.text: LOCAL REPORTER CONNECTS TWO UNRELATED THINGS — town unimpressed, string exhausted.
barks.speaker: gary

barks.text: Nope. Maybe after tea. Some strings only tie after tea.
barks.speaker: gary

barks.text: If that were true, half my other cards would be lying. Cards don't lie. Reporters just read them wrong.
barks.speaker: gary

barks.text: No. And the kettle whistled right when I tried it, which felt like an opinion.
barks.speaker: gary

barks.text: That's not a connection, that's two pins sharing a wall. Neighbors, not family.
barks.speaker: gary

barks.text: Mm. If I still believe that in the morning, I'll owe the board an apology.
barks.speaker: gary

barks.text: Ask the question under the question, Gibbons. That one was just the question on top.
barks.speaker: gary

barks.text: Not yet. Some pairs are true and still not ready. This one's neither.
barks.speaker: gary

barks.text: That's generous thinking. Generous like putting a hat on a lamppost.
barks.speaker: gary

barks.text: I can hear Dot already: 'Sell me that in one sentence.' I could not sell that in a paragraph.
barks.speaker: gary

barks.text: No. And I'm starting to think the string budget should count against me personally.
barks.speaker: gary
